CREATE TABLE test_timeout (id INT PRIMARY KEY, value VARCHAR(255));
SET MTS_2PC=1; SELECT mts_set_timeout(-1); INSERT INTO test_timeout (id, value) VALUES (3, 'test3');
SET MTS_2PC=1; SELECT mts_set_timeout(3000); UPDATE test_timeout SET value='updated' WHERE id=1;
SET MTS_2PC=1; SELECT mts_set_timeout(1000); DELETE FROM test_timeout WHERE id=2;
SET MTS_2PC=1; SELECT mts_set_timeout(0); INSERT INTO test_timeout (id, value) SELECT id + 10, CONCAT(value, '_new') FROM test_timeout WHERE id < 3;
SET MTS_2PC=1; SELECT mts_set_timeout(500); CREATE TABLE another_test (id INT, test_id INT, FOREIGN KEY (test_id) REFERENCES test_timeout(id));
SET MTS_2PC=1; SELECT mts_set_timeout(1000); INSERT INTO another_test (id, test_id) VALUES (1, 1);
SET MTS_2PC=1; SELECT mts_set_timeout(2000); SELECT * FROM test_timeout WHERE id IN (SELECT test_id FROM another_test);
